description: I was going to play recordings of Chuck-Will's-Widow and Norn was cueing up Eastern Whip-Poor-Will just for grins (hey, you never know).  Norm beat me and started the Whip-Poor-Will recording.  He played it for about 15 seconds and then stopped.  A responding Whip-Poor-Will was calling back from the dry creekbed area!!  We were all staring at each other, open mouthed in utter disbelief!  The bird called several phrases for about 10-12 seconds then stopped.  We got setup to record the bird with Merlin and Norm played the recording again, but alas, we could not elicit another song reponse.  About 15-20 minutes later we heard a "Quip" call further east, but was not sure it was the Whip-Poor-Will or a Common Poorwill.Bird called a rapidly repeating three parted "whip" "poor" "will" that  is diagnostic for Whip-poor-wills. This bird did NOT have the burry,  "stuttered" call of a Mexican Whip-poor-will.Our ebird checklist is located at: https://ebird.org/checklist/S118872146Loch Kilpatrick was able to get a recording of this bird on 10/20 and it can be found on his eBird checklist at:  https://ebird.org/checklist/S119166643
